metadata.task: Determine the geometric tipping angle of the block in degrees, ignoring sliding. Use the block half-width and half-height from the XML. Output one number rounded to the nearest tenth.
metadata.description: Work from the Tipping Point of a Block scene state rather than from the title alone. Query accessible object parameters and, when the task involves motion, collisions, forces, or oscillation, step the simulator and compare measured states before submitting the numeric answer.

expected_behavior: The LLM analyzes the torque and friction conditions to compute the critical angle based on the block’s geometry.
reasoning: The block has half-width 0.8 m and half-height 0.4 m, so tipping begins when tan(theta) = 0.8 / 0.4 = 2. Thus theta = atan(2) = 63.4 degrees.
